metaTitle: Config — Prosperity | Loot Overhaul for Minecraft
metaDescription: Configuration reference for Prosperity. Distance tiers, structure overrides, the container blacklist, indicators, and the prosperity.json file.
ogDescription: Config reference for prosperity.json with distance tiers, structure overrides, and more.
title: Configuration
lede: Every Prosperity setting, organized by section. Tune values in-game with Mod Menu, edit them by hand, or reload them live without a restart.

html: With Mod Menu and Cloth Config installed, every setting is editable from an in-game screen (Mods → Prosperity → Configure), with labels and tooltips per key. Saving writes the file and applies the change live; distance tiers, structure overrides, and the blacklist are edited as one row per entry. Both mods are optional — without them, edit the file directly.
The config file is auto-generated with defaults on first launch at:
Server operators can apply changes without a restart with:
Reloading re-reads the config from disk and syncs updated values to all connected clients.

sections.title: distanceTiers
sections.intro: Distance thresholds, quantity multipliers, and quality boosts for the five loot tiers. Stub — full key reference to follow.

blocks.note: Tier names match those used by loot injection (min_tier) and structure overrides.

sections.title: structureOverrides
sections.intro: Per-structure tier overrides with fixed, minimum, or maximum modes. Stub — see the structure override list in the spec.

html: Example: ocean monuments fixed to Wilderness, strongholds at minimum Outlands, village chests at maximum Frontier.

sections.title: Mob Loot Scaling
sections.intro: Apply the distance tiers to hostile-mob drops on player kills.

rows: Scale hostile-mob drops by the killer's distance tier.

html: When you land the killing blow on a hostile mob, its drops are scaled by the same distance tier used for containers — stack counts multiplied (capped at the item's max stack) and the tier's quality added to the loot luck. Passive mobs and environmental or non-player kills are never scaled. The Nether uses raw coordinates and the End is always the max tier, matching container scaling. This toggle is independent of distanceTiers scaling for containers.

sections.title: lootTableBlacklist
sections.intro: Opt-in list of loot tables exempt from all Prosperity behavior. Empty by default — every loot container is instanced.

html: Entries are loot-table ids, matched exactly (minecraft:chests/village/village_weaponsmith) or by trailing-* wildcard (somebigmod:* for a whole namespace, minecraft:chests/* for a subtree). Blacklisted containers fall back to vanilla loot behavior — distance scaling, injection, instancing, and the unlooted indicator do not apply.

sections.title: indicators
sections.intro: Client toggle for the unlooted-container sparkle overlay and related feedback.

html: Stub — keys for enabling the overlay, render distance, and the loot-tier action-bar message will be documented here.

sections.title: Tier HUD Badge
sections.intro: Client-side badge showing your current distance loot tier. These keys live in the client block of prosperity.json and are never synced.

rows: Screen corner: TOP_LEFT, TOP_RIGHT, BOTTOM_LEFT, BOTTOM_RIGHT.
Horizontal inset from the anchored corner, in pixels.
Vertical inset from the anchored corner, in pixels.

html: The badge shows a treasure-chest glyph and your current tier name, the name drawn in the tier's color; crossing a tier boundary briefly flashes the label gold. The tier is read from your position each frame using the server's synced tier boundaries (the Nether uses raw coordinates and the End is always the max tier, matching loot scaling). It is hidden while the GUI is hidden (F1), a screen is open, or you are spectating or dead. Prosperity holds slot 3 in the shared rfizzle HUD strip, so it stacks below Tribulation and Mercantile when those are installed.

sections.title: Loot Refresh
sections.intro: Optional per-player restocking so explored containers regenerate after a cooldown.

rows: Let a player's instanced loot regenerate after a cooldown.
In-game days before a player's loot refreshes (minimum 1).

html: Each player's cooldown is independent and measured from when they last generated that container's loot. Once it elapses, the unlooted sparkle reappears for that player and their next open rolls fresh loot from the original table at the current distance tier. The cooldown is checked lazily on interaction and on the indicator scan, so there is no per-tick cost. The generation time is recorded even while refresh is disabled, so enabling it later applies retroactively to already-looted containers.

sections.title: Container Protection
sections.intro: Optional anti-grief break resistance for instanced loot containers.

rows: Slow down breaking of loot containers that some online player still has pending.
How many times longer a protected container takes to break (clamped 1.0–100.0).

html: A protected container takes protectionBreakMultiplier× longer to break and plays a quiet cue when breaking starts, so a single player cannot quickly erase everyone else's instanced loot. Protection is a speed bump, not a wall: creative-mode players break instantly, and protection lifts once every online player has opened the container. Player-placed chests are never affected.
